title: cynoglossum lithospermifolium subsp. cariense
intro: Cynoglossum lithospermifolium subsp. cariense, also known as Cariense Hound's Tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is native to South Africa and is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Cariense Hound's Tongue have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
